subject: buffer() resolution keyword
truth: Stop using `resolution` in `buffer()`; use `quad_segs` instead.
quote: The `resolution` keyword to `buffer` has been deprecated to align with the convention in shapely, `quad_segs` should be used instead (#3600).

subject: sample_points() seed keyword
truth: Stop using `seed` in `sample_points`; use `rng` instead.
quote: Expired deprecations; option `use_pygeos` which had no functionality, `seed` keyword in sample_points (replaced by `rng`) (#3613)

subject: Minimum Python version
truth: GeoPandas 1.2 requires Python 3.11 or greater; code running on Python 3.10 or older will fail to install or import.
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and shapely 2.1, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: Minimum pandas version
truth: GeoPandas 1.2 requires pandas 2.2 or greater; older pandas versions are no longer supported.
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and shapely 2.1, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: Minimum numpy version
truth: GeoPandas 1.2 requires numpy 2.0 or greater; older numpy versions are no longer supported.
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and shapely 2.1, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: Minimum pyproj version
truth: GeoPandas 1.2 requires pyproj 3.7 or greater; older pyproj versions are no longer supported.
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and shapely 2.1, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: Minimum shapely version
truth: GeoPandas 1.2 requires shapely 2.1 or greater; older shapely versions are no longer supported.
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and shapely 2.1, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: use_pygeos option
truth: The `use_pygeos` option has been removed entirely; it had no functionality and setting it will cause an error.
quote: Expired deprecations; option `use_pygeos` which had no functionality, `seed` keyword in sample_points (replaced by `rng`) (#3613)

subject: Python version requirement
truth: GeoPandas 1.1 requires Python 3.10 or greater; code running on Python 3.9 or older will fail to install or import.
quote: GeoPandas 1.1 now requires Python 3.10 or greater

subject: pandas minimum version
truth: GeoPandas 1.1 requires pandas 2.0 or greater; code running on older pandas versions will break.
quote: pandas 2.0, numpy 1.24, pyproj 3.5, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: numpy minimum version
truth: GeoPandas 1.1 requires numpy 1.24 or greater; code running on older numpy versions will break.
quote: numpy 1.24, pyproj 3.5, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: pyproj minimum version
truth: GeoPandas 1.1 requires pyproj 3.5 or greater; code running on older pyproj versions will break.
quote: pyproj 3.5, are now the minimum required version for these dependencies.

subject: GeoSeries.select method
truth: Stop using GeoSeries.select() as it has been removed; the upstream pandas Series.select() no longer exists.
quote: The `GeoSeries.select` method wrapping the pandas `Series.select` method has been removed. The upstream method no longer exists in all supported version of pandas (#3394).

subject: geom_almost_equals method
truth: Stop using geom_almost_equals() as it has been removed; use geom_equals_exact() instead.
quote: The deprecated `geom_almost_equals` method has been removed. Use `geom_equals_exact` instead (#3522).

subject: shapely<2 and PyGEOS support
truth: GeoPandas 1.0 drops support for shapely<2 and PyGEOS; only shapely >= 2 is supported.
quote: GeoPandas 1.0 drops support for shapely<2 and PyGEOS. The only geometry engine that is currently supported is shapely >= 2.

subject: rtree-based spatial indexing
truth: Spatial indexing based on the rtree package has been removed.
quote: As a consequence, spatial indexing based on the rtree package has also been removed (#3035).

subject: I/O engine default
truth: The I/O engine now defaults to Pyogrio instead of Fiona.
quote: The I/O engine now defaults to Pyogrio which is now installed with GeoPandas instead of Fiona (#3223).

subject: align keyword in binary methods
new: align keyword defaults to None (treated as True)
truth: The `align` keyword in binary methods now defaults to `None` (treated as True); passing explicit True silences the warning about mismatched indices, but code relying on the old default may now see a warning.
quote: `align` keyword in binary methods now defaults to `None`, treated as True. Explicit True will silence the warning about mismatched indices (#3212).

from_fact: align keyword defaults to none (treated as true)

subject: GeoDataFrame constructor with named geometry argument
old: passing a named (Geo)Series as geometry argument could use that name as the active geometry column name
new: passing a named (Geo)Series as geometry argument always produces an active geometry column named 'geometry'
truth: Passing a named (Geo)Series as the `geometry` argument to the GeoDataFrame constructor now always produces an active geometry column named 'geometry', ignoring the Series name.
quote: Updated documentation to clarify that passing a named (Geo)Series as the `geometry` argument to the GeoDataFrame constructor will not use the name but will always produce a GeoDataFrame with an active geometry column named "geometry" (#3337).

from_fact: passing a named (geo)series as geometry argument could use that name as the active geometry column name
passing a named (geo)series as geometry argument always produces an active geometry column named 'geometry'

subject: overlay make_valid method
old: make_valid option in overlay used buffer(0)
new: make_valid option in overlay uses the make_valid method
truth: The `make_valid` option in `overlay` now uses the `make_valid` method instead of `buffer(0)`, which may produce different geometry repair results.
quote: `make_valid` option in `overlay` now uses the `make_valid` method instead of `buffer(0)` (#3113).

from_fact: make_valid option in overlay used buffer(0)
make_valid option in overlay uses the make_valid method

subject: sjoin index column name
old: sjoin always names the right index column 'index_right'
new: sjoin preserves the name of the right GeoDataFrame's index if it has one
truth: Stop assuming the right-index column from sjoin is always named 'index_right'; it may now be the original index name.
quote: The `sjoin` method will now preserve the name of the index of the right GeoDataFrame, if it has one, instead of always using `"index_right"` as the name for the resulting column in the return value

from_fact: sjoin always names the right index column 'index_right'
sjoin preserves the name of the right geodataframe's index if it has one

subject: passing unaligned Series to methods
old: passing an unaligned Series silently auto-aligned
new: passing an unaligned Series raises ValueError
truth: Stop passing unaligned Series as method arguments; GeoPandas now raises ValueError instead of silently auto-aligning.
quote: GeoPandas now raises a ValueError when an unaligned Series is passed as a method argument to avoid confusion of whether the automatic alignment happens or not

from_fact: passing an unaligned series silently auto-aligned
passing an unaligned series raises valueerror

subject: explode index_parts default
new: explode(..., index_parts=False) is now the default
truth: Stop relying on explode() defaulting to index_parts=True; the default is now False, matching pandas.
quote: The deprecated default value of GeoDataFrame/ GeoSeries `explode(.., index_parts=True)` is now set to false for consistency with pandas

from_fact: explode(..., index_parts=false) is now the default

subject: set_geometry with named Series
old: set_geometry(ser) kept the old active geometry column name
new: set_geometry(ser) uses ser.name as the new geometry column name if not None
truth: Stop assuming set_geometry(ser) keeps the old geometry column name; it now uses ser.name, which may create a second column if names differ.
quote: The behaviour of `set_geometry` has been changed when passed a (Geo)Series `ser` with a name. The new active geometry column name in this case will be `ser.name`, if not None, rather than the previous active geometry column name.

from_fact: set_geometry(ser) kept the old active geometry column name
set_geometry(ser) uses ser.name as the new geometry column name if not none

subject: delaunay_triangles element-wise vs all
truth: Stop relying on delaunay_triangles operating element-wise per geometry; it now triangulates all geometries together. Use shapely.delaunay_triangles for per-geometry behaviour.
quote: `delaunay_triangles` now considers all geometries together when creating the Delaunay triangulation instead of performing the operation element-wise.

subject: read_file with no geometry field
old: read_file returned a GeoDataFrame with an empty geometry column when source had no geometry
new: read_file returns a plain pandas DataFrame when source has no geometry field
truth: Stop expecting read_file to always return a GeoDataFrame; if the source has no geometry field, it now returns a plain pandas DataFrame.
quote: Reading a data source that does not have a geometry field using `read_file` now returns a Pandas DataFrame instead of a GeoDataFrame with an empty `geometry` column.

from_fact: read_file returned a geodataframe with an empty geometry column when source had no geometry
read_file returns a plain pandas dataframe when source has no geometry field

subject: geopandas.datasets module
truth: Stop using geopandas.datasets; use the geodatasets package instead.
quote: The I/O engine now defaults to Pyogrio which is now installed with GeoPandas instead

subject: geopandas.io.read_file, geopandas.io.to_file, geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis
truth: Stop using geopandas.io.read_file, geopandas.io.to_file, and geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis; use geopandas.read_file, geopandas.read_postgis, and GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries.to_file() instead.
quote: Removed deprecated functions geopandas.io.read_file, geopandas.io.to_file and geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis. geopandas.read_file, geopandas.read_postgis and the GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries to_file(..) method should be used instead.

subject: GeometryArray.data property
truth: Stop using GeometryArray.data; use np.asarray() or the to_numpy() method instead.
quote: Removed deprecated GeometryArray.data property, np.asarray(..) or the to_numpy() method should be used instead.

subject: sindex.query_bulk method
truth: Stop using sindex.query_bulk; use sindex.query instead.
quote: Removed deprecated sindex.query_bulk method, using sindex.query instead.

subject: sjoin parameter 'op'
truth: Stop using the 'op' parameter in sjoin; use the 'predicate' parameter instead.
quote: Removed deprecated sjoin parameter op, predicate should be supplied instead.

subject: GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame __xor__, __or__, __and__, __sub__ operators
truth: Stop using ^, |, &, - operators on GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame; use symmetric_difference(), union(), intersection(), difference() methods instead.
quote: Removed deprecated GeoSeries/ GeoDataFrame methods __xor__, __or__, __and__ and __sub__. Instead use methods symmetric_difference, union, intersection and difference respectively.

subject: plot_polygon_collection, plot_linestring_collection, plot_point_collection functions
truth: Stop using plot_polygon_collection, plot_linestring_collection, plot_point_collection; use GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame .plot() method directly instead.
quote: Removed deprecated plotting functions plot_polygon_collection, plot_linestring_collection and plot_point_collection, use the GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame .plot method directly instead.

subject: GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame .plot parameters 'axes' and 'colormap'
truth: Stop using 'axes' and 'colormap' parameters in .plot(); use 'ax' and 'cmap' instead.
quote: Removed deprecated GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame .plot parameters axes and colormap, instead use ax and cmap respectively.

subject: to_parquet and to_feather 'version' keyword
truth: The 'version' keyword in to_parquet and to_feather is no longer used for GeoParquet spec version; use 'schema_version' instead. The 'version' keyword now passes through to pyarrow.
quote: Removed compatibility for specifying the version keyword in to_parquet and to_feather. This keyword will now be passed through to pyarrow and use schema_version to specify the GeoParquet specification version

subject: unary_union attribute
truth: Stop using the unary_union attribute; use the union_all() method instead.
quote: unary_union attribute is now deprecated and replaced by the union_all() method

subject: set_geometry drop keyword
truth: Stop using the drop keyword in set_geometry(); remove drop=False calls (default is same), and replace drop=True with gdf.set_geometry(new_geo_col).drop(columns=geo_col_name).rename_geometry(geo_col_name).
quote: gdf.set_geometry(new_geo_col).drop(columns=geo_col_name).rename_geometry(geo_col_name)

subject: geopandas.use_pygeos option
truth: Stop using geopandas.use_pygeos; it is deprecated and will be removed in GeoPandas 1.1.
quote: The geopandas.use_pygeos option has been deprecated and will be removed in GeoPandas 1.1

subject: Manual override of crs property on GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame
truth: Stop manually overriding CRS by setting the crs property directly; use the set_crs() method instead.
quote: Manual overriding of an existing CRS of a GeoSeries or GeoDataFrame by setting the `crs` property has been deprecated

subject: GeoPandas default geometry backend
old: PyGEOS used by default when both Shapely >= 2.0 and PyGEOS are installed
new: Shapely 2.0 used by default when both Shapely >= 2.0 and PyGEOS are installed
truth: GeoPandas 0.14 uses Shapely 2.0 by default instead of PyGEOS when both Shapely >= 2.0 and PyGEOS are installed; PyGEOS is only default with Shapely < 2.0.
quote: GeoPandas will use Shapely 2.0 by default instead of PyGEOS when both Shapely >= 2.0 and PyGEOS are installed. PyGEOS will continue to be used by default when PyGEOS is installed alongside Shapely < 2.0. Support for PyGEOS and Shapely < 2.0 will be removed in GeoPandas 1.0.

from_fact: pygeos used by default when both shapely >= 2.0 and pygeos are installed
shapely 2.0 used by default when both shapely >= 2.0 and pygeos are installed

subject: sample_points keyword
truth: Stop using 'seed' keyword in sample_points; use 'rng' instead.
quote: `seed` keyword in sample_points (replaced by `rng`) (#3613)

subject: Python 3.8 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.14 drops support for Python 3.8.
quote: GeoPandas 0.14 drops support for Python 3.8

subject: pandas 1.3 and below support
truth: GeoPandas 0.14 drops support for pandas 1.3 and below; minimum supported pandas version is now 1.4.
quote: GeoPandas 0.14 drops support for Python 3.8 and pandas 1.3 and below (the minimum

subject: geom_almost_equals() method
truth: Stop using geom_almost_equals() and use geom_equals_exact() instead.
quote: geom_almost_equals() methods have been deprecated and geom_equals_exact() should be used instead

subject: query_bulk() method of spatial index
truth: Stop using sindex.query_bulk() and use sindex.query() instead.
quote: The query_bulk() method of the spatial index .sindex property is deprecated in favor of query()

subject: unary_union return value for all-None GeoSeries
old: unary_union returns None for all-None GeoSeries
new: unary_union returns 'GEOMETRYCOLLECTION EMPTY' for all-None GeoSeries
truth: unary_union on an all-None GeoSeries will now return 'GEOMETRYCOLLECTION EMPTY' instead of None.
quote: unary_union will return 'GEOMETRYCOLLECTION EMPTY' instead of None for all-None GeoSeries

from_fact: unary_union returns none for all-none geoseries
unary_union returns 'geometrycollection empty' for all-none geoseries

subject: GeoDataFrame creation from DataFrame
old: Creating a GeoDataFrame from a DataFrame could produce a view (mutating one would affect the other)
new: Creating a GeoDataFrame from a DataFrame now always produces a copy
truth: GeoDataFrame created from a DataFrame is now a copy, not a view; mutating it will no longer affect the source DataFrame.
quote: Ensure that GeoDataFrame created from DataFrame is a copy, not a view (#2667)

from_fact: creating a geodataframe from a dataframe could produce a view (mutating one would affect the other)
creating a geodataframe from a dataframe now always produces a copy

subject: Minimum pandas version
truth: GeoPandas 0.13 drops support for pandas 1.0.5; minimum pandas version is now 1.1.
quote: GeoPandas 0.13 drops support pandas 1.0.5 (the minimum supported pandas version is now 1.1).

subject: Minimum shapely version
truth: GeoPandas 0.13 requires shapely >= 1.7.1; older shapely versions are no longer supported.
quote: the minimum required versions for the listed dependencies have now changed to shapely 1.7.1

subject: Minimum fiona version
truth: GeoPandas 0.13 requires fiona >= 1.8.19; older fiona versions are no longer supported.
quote: The upstream method no longer exists in all supported version of pandas (#3394).

subject: Minimum pyproj version
truth: GeoPandas 0.13 requires pyproj >= 3.0.1; older pyproj versions are no longer supported.
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and

subject: Minimum matplotlib version
truth: GeoPandas 0.13 requires matplotlib >= 3.3.4; older matplotlib versions are no longer supported.
quote: GeoPandas now requires pandas >=0.23.4 and matplotlib >=2.0.1 (#1002).

subject: Accessing crs on GeoDataFrame without active geometry column
old: Accessing .crs on a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column was deprecated but still worked
new: Accessing .crs on a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column now raises an AttributeError
truth: Accessing the `crs` attribute of a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column now raises an AttributeError instead of returning a value.
quote: Accessing the `crs` of a `GeoDataFrame` without active geometry column was deprecated and this now raises an AttributeError (#2578).

from_fact: accessing .crs on a geodataframe without an active geometry column was deprecated but still worked
accessing .crs on a geodataframe without an active geometry column now raises an attributeerror

subject: Accessing .geometry when active geometry column is missing but 'geometry' column exists
old: gdf.geometry returned gdf['geometry'] when active geometry column was missing but a column named 'geometry' existed
new: gdf.geometry now raises an AttributeError when active geometry column is missing, even if a column named 'geometry' exists
truth: Accessing `gdf.geometry` when the active geometry column is missing but a column named 'geometry' is present now raises an AttributeError instead of returning that column.
quote: Accessing `gdf.geometry` where the active geometry column is missing, and a column named `"geometry"` is present will now raise an `AttributeError`, rather than returning `gdf["geometry"]` (#2575).

from_fact: gdf.geometry returned gdf['geometry'] when active geometry column was missing but a column named 'geometry' existed
gdf.geometry now raises an attributeerror when active geometry column is missing, even if a column named 'geometry' exists

subject: GeometryArray.equals_exact()
truth: Stop using GeometryArray.equals_exact(); use GeometryArray.geom_equals_exact() instead.
quote: Deprecated methods GeometryArray.equals_exact() and GeometryArray.almost_equals() have been removed. They should be replaced with GeometryArray.geom_equals_exact() and GeometryArray.geom_almost_equals() respectively (#2267).

subject: GeometryArray.almost_equals()
truth: Stop using GeometryArray.almost_equals(); use GeometryArray.geom_almost_equals() instead.
quote: Deprecated methods GeometryArray.equals_exact() and GeometryArray.almost_equals() have been removed. They should be replaced with GeometryArray.geom_equals_exact() and GeometryArray.geom_almost_equals() respectively (#2267).

subject: CRS functions explicit_crs_from_epsg(), epsg_from_crs(), get_epsg_file_contents()
truth: Stop using explicit_crs_from_epsg(), epsg_from_crs(), and get_epsg_file_contents(); they have been removed.
quote: Deprecated CRS functions explicit_crs_from_epsg(), epsg_from_crs() and get_epsg_file_contents() were removed (#2340).

subject: GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries constructor with contradictory CRS
old: Contradictory CRS silently accepted or behaved differently
truth: Specifying a CRS in the GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries constructor that contradicts the underlying GeometryArray now raises a ValueError.
quote: Specifying a CRS in the GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries constructor which contradicted the underlying GeometryArray now raises a ValueError (#2100).

from_fact: contradictory crs silently accepted or behaved differently

subject: Writing an empty GeoDataFrame to file
truth: Writing an empty GeoDataFrame to a file now emits a UserWarning instead of raising a ValueError.
quote: It is now possible to write an empty GeoDataFrame to a file for supported formats (#2240). Attempting to do so will now emit a UserWarning instead of a ValueError.

subject: DeprecationWarning → FutureWarning for set operations and io functions
truth: GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame set operations (__xor__, __or__, __and__, __sub__) and geopandas.io.file.read_file/to_file and geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis now emit FutureWarning instead of DeprecationWarning.
quote: Deprecated GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame set operations __xor__(), __or__(), __and__() and __sub__(), geopandas.io.file.read_file/to_file and geopandas.io.sql.read_postgis now emit FutureWarning instead of DeprecationWarning and will be completely removed in a future release.

subject: Accessing crs on GeoDataFrame without active geometry column
truth: Accessing the crs of a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column is deprecated and will be removed in GeoPandas 0.12.
quote: Accessing the crs of a GeoDataFrame without active geometry column is deprecated and will be removed in GeoPandas 0.12 (#2373).

subject: Python 3.7 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 drops support for Python 3.7
quote: GeoPandas 0.11 drops support for Python 3.7

subject: pandas 0.25 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 drops support for pandas 0.25; minimum supported pandas version is now 1.0.5
quote: GeoPandas 0.11 drops support for Python 3.7 and pandas 0.25 (the minimum supported pandas version is now 1.0.5)

subject: shapely < 1.7 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 requires shapely >= 1.7
quote: the minimum required versions for the listed dependencies have now changed to shapely 1.7

subject: fiona < 1.8.13.post1 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 requires fiona >= 1.8.13.post1
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and

subject: pyproj < 2.6.1.post1 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 requires pyproj >= 2.6.1.post1
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and

subject: matplotlib < 3.2 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 requires matplotlib >= 3.2
quote: GeoPandas now requires pandas >=0.23.4 and matplotlib >=2.0.1 (#1002).

subject: mapclassify < 2.4.0 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.11 requires mapclassify >= 2.4.0
quote: GeoPandas 1.2 now requires Python 3.11 or greater and pandas 2.2, numpy 2.0, pyproj 3.7, and

subject: sjoin() op= keyword
truth: Stop using op= in sjoin(), use predicate= instead.
quote: The op= keyword of sjoin() to indicate which spatial predicate to use for joining is being deprecated and renamed in favor of a new predicate= keyword (#1626).

subject: cascaded_union attribute
truth: Stop using cascaded_union, use unary_union instead.
quote: The cascaded_union attribute is deprecated, use unary_union instead (#2074).

subject: GeoDataFrame construction with duplicated geometry column
truth: Constructing a GeoDataFrame with a duplicated 'geometry' column now raises an error.
quote: Constructing a GeoDataFrame with a duplicated "geometry" column is now

subject: explode() default index behaviour
new: will change in future; use index_parts keyword to control
truth: explode() currently returns MultiIndex with part indices but this will change; use index_parts keyword to control behaviour.
quote: The explode() method currently returns a GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame with a MultiIndex, with an additional level with indices of the parts of the exploded multi-geometries. For consistency with pandas, this will change in the future and the new index_parts keyword is added to control this.

from_fact: will change in future; use index_parts keyword to control

subject: clip() function
old: clip() left MultiPoints intact when partly outside clip bounds
new: clip() correctly clips MultiPoints that are partly outside clip bounds
truth: clip() now correctly clips MultiPoints that are partly outside the clip bounds instead of leaving them intact
quote: leaving them intact when partly outside of the clip bounds (#2148).

from_fact: clip() left multipoints intact when partly outside clip bounds
clip() correctly clips multipoints that are partly outside clip bounds

subject: GeoDataFrame constructor with GeoDataFrame argument
old: GeoDataFrame(gdf) lost the geometry column name
new: GeoDataFrame(gdf) now preserves the geometry column name
truth: GeoDataFrame(gdf) now preserves the geometry column name instead of losing it when the argument is already a GeoDataFrame
quote: Fix geometry column name propagation when GeoDataFrame columns are a multiindex (#2088)

from_fact: geodataframe(gdf) lost the geometry column name
geodataframe(gdf) now preserves the geometry column name

subject: GeoDataFrame.__setitem__ (setting column values)
old: Setting values as a column lost the CRS of those values
new: Setting values as a column now preserves the CRS
truth: Setting values as a column via GeoDataFrame.__setitem__ now preserves the CRS of those values instead of losing it
quote: Fix loss of the values' CRS when setting those values as a column

from_fact: setting values as a column lost the crs of those values
setting values as a column now preserves the crs

subject: Python 3.6 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 dropped support for Python 3.6
quote: GeoPandas 0.10.0 dropped support for Python 3.6 and pandas 0.24.

subject: pandas 0.24 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 dropped support for pandas 0.24
quote: GeoPandas 0.10.0 dropped support for Python 3.6 and pandas 0.24.

subject: Minimum required numpy version
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required numpy version to 1.18
quote: Further, the minimum required versions are numpy 1.18, shapely 1.6, fiona 1.8, matplotlib 3.1 and pyproj 2.2.

subject: Minimum required shapely version
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required shapely version to 1.6
quote: Further, the minimum required versions are numpy 1.18, shapely 1.6, fiona 1.8, matplotlib 3.1 and pyproj 2.2.

subject: Minimum required fiona version
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required fiona version to 1.8
quote: Further, the minimum required versions are numpy 1.18, shapely 1.6, fiona 1.8, matplotlib 3.1 and pyproj 2.2.

subject: Minimum required matplotlib version
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required matplotlib version to 3.1
quote: Further, the minimum required versions are numpy 1.18, shapely 1.6, fiona 1.8, matplotlib 3.1 and pyproj 2.2.

subject: Minimum required pyproj version
truth: GeoPandas 0.10.0 raised the minimum required pyproj version to 2.2
quote: Further, the minimum required versions are numpy 1.18, shapely 1.6, fiona 1.8, matplotlib 3.1 and pyproj 2.2.

subject: mapclassify version requirement for plotting with classification schema
old: Plotting with a classification schema worked with mapclassify < 2.4
new: Plotting with a classification schema now requires mapclassify >= 2.4
truth: Plotting with a classification schema now requires mapclassify version >= 2.4
quote: Plotting with a classification schema now requires mapclassify version >= 2.4 (#1737).

from_fact: plotting with a classification schema worked with mapclassify < 2.4
plotting with a classification schema now requires mapclassify >= 2.4

subject: GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame construction with mismatched CRS
new: deprecation warning raised, will become error in future
truth: Constructing a GeoSeries or GeoDataFrame from data with a different CRS now raises a deprecation warning; use set_crs() to override an existing CRS.
quote: When constructing a GeoSeries or GeoDataFrame from data that already has a CRS, a deprecation warning is raised when both CRS don't match, and in the future an error will be raised in such a case.

from_fact: deprecation warning raised, will become error in future

subject: set operators (&, |, ^, -) on GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame
truth: The set operators (&, |, ^, -) on GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame are deprecated; use intersection(), union(), symmetric_difference(), difference() methods instead.
quote: Instead use methods `symmetric_difference`, `union`, `intersection` and

subject: .crs attribute type
truth: The .crs attribute of GeoSeries and GeoDataFrame now stores a pyproj.CRS object instead of a proj4 string or dict.
quote: GeoDataFrame no longer stores the CRS information as a proj4 string or dict,

subject: GeoDataFrame.to_file index writing
old: did not write index to file
new: writes index to file if index is named and/or non-integer
truth: GeoDataFrame.to_file() now writes the index to the file when the index is named and/or non-integer; use index=True/False to control this.
quote: to the file, if the index is named and/or non-integer.

from_fact: did not write index to file
writes index to file if index is named and/or non-integer

subject: Python 2.7 support
truth: GeoPandas 0.7.0 dropped support for Python 2.7; Python >= 3.5 is now required.
quote: Support for Python 2.7 has been dropped. GeoPandas now works with Python >= 3.5.

subject: PROJ and pyproj version requirements
truth: GeoPandas 0.7.0 now requires PROJ > 6 and pyproj > 2.2.
quote: GeoPandas now requires PROJ > 6 and pyproj > 2.2

subject: GeoSeries.dtype
truth: GeoSeries.dtype is now 'geometry' dtype instead of numpy object dtype.
quote: The .dtype of a GeoSeries is now a 'geometry' dtype (and no longer a numpy object dtype).

subject: GeoSeries.values
truth: GeoSeries.values now returns a GeometryArray instead of a numpy array; use np.asarray() to get a numpy array of Shapely scalars.
quote: The .values of a GeoSeries now returns a custom GeometryArray, and no longer a numpy array. To get back a numpy array of Shapely scalars, you can convert explicitly using np.asarray(..).

subject: Missing value handling in GeoSeries
old: missing geometries and empty geometries were conflated
new: missing geometries and empty geometries are now separate concepts
truth: Missing geometries and empty geometries are now separate concepts, changing how None/NaN vs empty geometry objects behave.
quote: The missing value handling has been changed to now separate the concepts of missing geometries and empty geometries (#601, 1062).

from_fact: missing geometries and empty geometries were conflated
missing geometries and empty geometries are now separate concepts

subject: GeoSeries.isna
old: considered both missing values and empty geometries
truth: GeoSeries.isna no longer considers empty geometries as missing; use GeoSeries.is_empty to check for empty geometries.
quote: GeoSeries.isna now considers only missing values, and if you want to check for empty geometries, you can use GeoSeries.is_empty

from_fact: considered both missing values and empty geometries

subject: GeoSeries.dropna
old: did not drop missing or empty geometries
truth: GeoSeries.dropna now actually drops missing values (previously it dropped nothing).
quote: GeoSeries.dropna now actually drops missing values (before it didn't drop either missing or empty geometries)

from_fact: did not drop missing or empty geometries

subject: GeoSeries.align
old: used empty geometries to fill non-matching index entries
new: uses missing values (NaN) instead of empty geometries
truth: GeoSeries.align now fills non-matching index entries with missing values instead of empty geometries.
quote: GeoSeries.align uses missing values instead of empty geometries by default to fill non-matching index entries.

from_fact: used empty geometries to fill non-matching index entries
uses missing values (nan) instead of empty geometries

subject: plot method
truth: Stop using `axes=` in plot(); use `ax=` instead.
quote: Removed deprecated GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame `.plot` parameters `axes` and `colormap`, instead use

subject: plot method
truth: Stop using `colormap=` in plot(); use `cmap=` instead.
quote: Removed deprecated GeoSeries/GeoDataFrame `.plot` parameters `axes` and `colormap`, instead use

subject: read_file() parameters include_fields and ignore_fields
truth: Stop using include_fields and ignore_fields in read_file(); use the columns keyword instead.
quote: Added support to ``read_file`` for the ``columns`` keyword for the fiona engine (#3133).

subject: pandas.concat of GeoSeries/GeoDataFrames with different CRS
old: pandas.concat of GeoSeries/GeoDataFrames with different CRS would silently override CRS information
new: pandas.concat of GeoSeries/GeoDataFrames with different CRS now raises an error or warns (no longer silently overrides)
truth: Combining GeoSeries/GeoDataFrames with pandas.concat will no longer silently override CRS information if not all inputs have the same CRS.
quote: Combining GeoSeries/GeoDataFrames with ``pandas.concat`` will no longer silently

from_fact: pandas.concat of geoseries/geodataframes with different crs would silently override crs information
pandas.concat of geoseries/geodataframes with different crs now raises an error or warns (no longer silently overrides)

subject: to_parquet and to_feather version parameter
truth: Stop using the 'version' parameter in to_parquet and to_feather; use 'schema_version' instead, as 'version' is now passed directly to the underlying writer.
quote: ``version`` will be passed directly to underlying

subject: GeoDataFrame constructor with no geometry column + set_crs without active geometry column
truth: Specifying a CRS in the GeoDataFrame constructor when no geometry column is provided, and calling set_crs on a GeoDataFrame without an active geometry column, now raise a ValueError.
quote: Specifying a CRS in the ``GeoDataFrame`` constructor when no geometry column is provided

subject: GeoSeries constructor with non-geometry data
truth: Passing non-geometry data to the GeoSeries constructor now raises a TypeError instead of returning a pandas.Series.
quote: Passing non-geometry data to the``GeoSeries`` constructor is now fully deprecated and

subject: GeoSeries.[xyz] behaviour with empty points
old: GeoSeries.[xyz] raised IndexError when GeoSeries contained empty points
new: Rows corresponding to empty points now contain np.nan
truth: GeoSeries.x, GeoSeries.y, and GeoSeries.z now return np.nan for empty points instead of raising an IndexError
quote: Rows corresponding to empty points now contain ``np.nan``.

from_fact: geoseries.[xyz] raised indexerror when geoseries contained empty points
rows corresponding to empty points now contain np.nan

subject: GeoSeries.isna() on empty GeoSeries
old: GeoSeries.isna() on an empty GeoSeries did not return a boolean Series
new: GeoSeries.isna() on an empty GeoSeries now correctly returns a boolean Series
truth: GeoSeries.isna() on an empty GeoSeries now correctly returns a boolean Series instead of a non-boolean result
quote: Fix ``GeoSeries.isna()`` to correctly return a boolean Series in case of an

from_fact: geoseries.isna() on an empty geoseries did not return a boolean series
geoseries.isna() on an empty geoseries now correctly returns a boolean series

subject: GeoDataFrame.apply()
old: GeoDataFrame.apply() lost the active geometry column name
new: GeoDataFrame.apply() now preserves the active geometry column name
truth: GeoDataFrame.apply() now preserves the active geometry column name instead of losing it
quote: Fix in ``GeoDataFrame.apply()`` to preserve the active geometry column name

from_fact: geodataframe.apply() lost the active geometry column name
geodataframe.apply() now preserves the active geometry column name

subject: sjoin() with right join (how='right')
old: sjoin() ignored the suffixes parameter when how='right'
new: sjoin() now respects the suffixes parameter for right joins
truth: sjoin() now respects the suffixes parameter when doing a right join (how='right') instead of ignoring it
quote: Fix in ``sjoin()`` to not ignore the suffixes in case of a right-join

from_fact: sjoin() ignored the suffixes parameter when how='right'
sjoin() now respects the suffixes parameter for right joins

subject: GeoDataFrame.explode() with MultiIndex
old: GeoDataFrame.explode() with a MultiIndex did not work correctly
new: GeoDataFrame.explode() now works correctly with a MultiIndex
truth: GeoDataFrame.explode() now works correctly with a MultiIndex instead of failing or producing incorrect results
quote: ``GeoSeries.reset_index()`` now correctly results in a GeoDataFrame instead of DataFrame (#1252).

from_fact: geodataframe.explode() with a multiindex did not work correctly
geodataframe.explode() now works correctly with a multiindex

subject: to_file() and to_json() with duplicate columns
old: to_file() and to_json() did not raise an error when DataFrame had duplicate columns
new: to_file() and to_json() now raise an error when DataFrame has duplicate columns
truth: to_file() and to_json() now raise an error when the DataFrame has duplicate columns instead of silently producing incorrect output
quote: Fix ``to_file()`` and ``to_json()`` when DataFrame has duplicate columns to

from_fact: to_file() and to_json() did not raise an error when dataframe had duplicate columns
to_file() and to_json() now raise an error when dataframe has duplicate columns

subject: GeoDataFrame.explode() attrs preservation
truth: GeoDataFrame.explode() now preserves the attrs attribute instead of dropping it
quote: Fix ``GeoDataFrame.explode()`` to preserve ``attrs`` (#1935)

subject: is_ring attribute on Polygons
new: is_ring will return False for Polygons (future change)
truth: Stop relying on is_ring returning True for Polygon geometries; it will return False in a future version.
quote: The ``is_ring`` attribute currently returns True for Polygons.

from_fact: is_ring will return false for polygons (future change)

subject: objects keyword in sindex.intersection()
truth: Stop using the 'objects' keyword in the intersection() method of the spatial index (sindex); it has been removed.
quote: ``GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries.sindex`` spatial index object has been removed

old: publicly accessible helper functions in geopandas.plotting module
truth: The helper functions in the geopandas.plotting module are deprecated for public usage and should not be imported.
quote: The helper functions in the ``geopandas.plotting`` module are deprecated for

from_fact: publicly accessible helper functions in geopandas.plotting module

subject: geopandas.io functions
truth: The geopandas.io functions are deprecated; use top-level read_file() and to_file() instead.
quote: The ``geopandas.io`` functions are deprecated, use the top-level ``read_file`` and

subject: sindex for empty dataframe
truth: The sindex attribute on an empty GeoDataFrame/GeoSeries will in the future return an empty spatial index instead of None.
quote: The spatial index of a GeoSeries (accessed with the ``sindex`` attribute) is

subject: objects keyword in sindex.intersection()
truth: The 'objects' keyword in the intersection method of the spatial index (sindex) is deprecated and will be removed.
quote: The ``objects`` keyword in the ``intersection`` method of the spatial index

associative: get objects from spatial index query

subject: GeoSeries constructor with non-geometry data
new: raises a warning, will raise an error in future
truth: GeoSeries constructor now raises a warning when passed non-geometry data; in the future this will raise an error.
quote: The ``GeoSeries`` constructor now raises a warning when passed non-geometry data.

from_fact: raises a warning, will raise an error in future

subject: default geocoding provider
truth: Stop relying on Google as the default geocoding provider; the default is now Geocode.Farm.
quote: Changed the default provider in ``geopandas.tools.geocode`` from Google (now requires an API key) to Geocode.Farm (#907, #975).

subject: choropleth dependency
old: PySAL (for choropleth mapping with scheme keyword)
truth: Stop relying on PySAL for choropleth mapping with the scheme keyword; the dependency has switched to mapclassify.
quote: dependency for choropleth mapping with the ``scheme`` keyword (#872).

from_fact: pysal (for choropleth mapping with scheme keyword)
